title: malus gratissima
intro: Malus gratissima, also known as the Manchurian apple or Chinese crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to northeastern Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Malus gratissima has been reported to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine formulations for its various health benefits.
